---
title: "Module Library"
---

# Reusable teaching modules

These pages **mirror the week’s arc** but live outside the slide decks: you can skim them before class, point students to them for refresher reading, or grow them into longer notes over time.

The course uses **two data tracks**: synthetic **genes** (see the gene notebooks) and **[Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)** for real-data continuity.

| Module | Day focus | Role |
|--------|-----------|------|
| [01 — Foundations](#module-01-foundations-and-generalization) | Monday | Splits, bias–variance, data card |
| [02 — Regularization](#module-02-regularization) | Monday | Ridge, lasso, AIC, elastic net |
| [03 — Trees & cultures](#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures) | Tuesday | Two cultures, tree intuition |
| [04 — Tidymodels pipeline](#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline) | Tuesday | Full `tidymodels` walk-through (includes shared code) |
| [05 — Advanced models](#module-05-advanced-models) | Thursday | RF, boosting, NNs at API level |
| [06 — Metrics & SHAP](#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap) | Thursday | Metrics, imbalance, interpretability |

---

## Module 01: Foundations and Generalization {#module-01-foundations-and-generalization}

Supervised learning setup, **train / validation / test**, bias–variance language, and a lightweight **data card** habit. Ties the **gene** simulations (known truth) to **Palmer Penguins** (real measurements) so students see the same ideas on two tables.

**[Open Module 01 →](module-01-foundations.qmd)**

---

## Module 02: Regularization {#module-02-regularization}

Ridge and lasso as **continuous shrinkage**, AIC / stepwise as **discrete** search, and when **elastic net** is a sensible middle ground. Links Monday slides to both the **gene LASSO** notebook and **penguin mass** ridge/LASSO practice.

**[Open Module 02 →](module-02-regularization.qmd)**

---

## Module 03: Trees and Two Cultures {#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures}

Statistical vs algorithmic cultures at a high level, then **decision tree** mechanics (splits, impurity, depth) connected to the **penguin** classification notebooks and the Tuesday slide deck.

**[Open Module 03 →](module-03-trees-and-cultures.qmd)**

---

## Module 04: Tidymodels Pipeline {#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline}

The **canonical code path**: a full **Palmer Penguins** (Adelie vs Gentoo) `tidymodels` workflow embedded via `{{< include >}}` from `_includes/tidymodels-pipeline.qmd` — same content as the paced block on **Tuesday** slides and the **penguins-classification** notebook story.

**[Open Module 04 →](module-04-pipeline.qmd)**

---

## Module 05: Advanced Models {#module-05-advanced-models}

Random forests, boosting, and neural nets as **higher-capacity** learners; emphasis on **keeping the same validation discipline** and swapping **`mod_spec`** inside the Module 04 skeleton.

**[Open Module 05 →](module-05-advanced-models.qmd)**

---

## Module 06: Metrics, Imbalance, and SHAP {#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap}

Metric choice tied to **costs**, class imbalance without accuracy tunnel vision, and **SHAP** with causal guardrails. Points to **Thursday** slides and **multiclass / sex** penguin notebooks for concrete confusion matrices.

**[Open Module 06 →](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd)**
